Out of all the concepts I have created,
SYNTHA is probably the most ambitious.
Rather than being a single game, SYNTHA
is the idea of a larger universe that
connects multiple stories and worlds
together.
Concepts such as Threadweaver, Archive
Zero, and several other planned projects
all contribute pieces to this larger
narrative.
The idea behind SYNTHA is that every
story matters.
Events, locations, and characters from
different games eventually become part of
something much bigger.
While it currently exists mostly as notes,
worldbuilding, and planning documents, it
is the project I am most excited about
developing in the future.
While SYNTHA represents the long-term
goal, Archive Zero is one of the projects
I would like to create along the way.
Archive Zero is a concept centred around
mysterious cases, strange events, and
unsolved stories from different points in
history.
Players would experience these cases
firsthand and slowly uncover the truth
behind them.
What excites me most about the project is
the atmosphere and storytelling
potential.
It would allow me to experiment with
mystery, horror, and historical themes in
ways that would not fit within
Threadweaver.
Looking to the future, my goal is to
continue developing concepts like these
and gradually turn them into real
projects.
Every world starts as an idea, and these
concepts are the first step towards
creating the experiences I want to share
with others.
Congrats you officially know how to walk through my
portfolio, here are some pointers for up ahead
this portfolio is divided into 4 worlds,
Every single world has 4-5 signs with information
about me, some are harder to find then others, so try
to find them all
Red Icon
Interactable signs are indicated with red
sigils, like the ones shown here.
Green Icon
Interactions with the world however are
marked with green sigils, don't worry
there's plenty you'll find!
As an extra note for my assessors, all of the signs
that are in this game can also be found right here
on this plaza. every sign also has a prop near them to
indicate which area it's from
Threadweaver is currently my largest game
concept.
The story takes place in a world where
time has been frozen and follows
characters trying to uncover the truth
behind what happened.
Over time, the project grew far beyond
its original scope and now includes
multiple regions, characters, factions,
and story arcs.
Building this world taught me how much I
enjoy worldbuilding and storytelling.
Even though the project is still far from
complete, it remains one of the ideas I
am most passionate about.
One of my favourite things to do is
come up with game concepts.
Even when I'm not actively developing a
game, I'm usually thinking about worlds,
stories, mechanics, or characters.
Many of the ideas in this area started as
small thoughts that gradually grew into
much larger concepts.
Some of them may never become full games,
but they all help me develop my creativity
and storytelling skills.
Hi! My name is Tim.
One of the easiest ways to describe me is as someone who
loves stories, games, and creative projects.
I've always enjoyed exploring fictional worlds and
seeing how different characters, locations, and events
connect together.
A lot of my hobbies revolve around that.
Whether it's playing games, listening to music,
watching movies, or coming up with my own ideas,
I'm usually interested in things that tell a story
or create a memorable experience.
Games are probably my biggest hobby.
My favourite game series is Kingdom
Hearts.
I also love Xenoblade Chronicles, Mario &
Luigi, Fortnite, and Clair Obscur:
Expedition 33.
What I enjoy most about these games is
their worldbuilding, characters, music,
and stories.
I love discovering new locations,
learning about the lore, and seeing how
different storylines connect together.
They are also a big source of inspiration
for many of my own ideas and concepts.
Besides games, I also spend a lot of
time watching movies and series.
My favourite series are Game of Thrones
and Andor.
I enjoy stories with interesting worlds,
memorable characters, and plenty of lore
to dive into.
My favourite movies are Kill Bill Vol. 1,
Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the
Sith, and Star Wars Episode V: The Empire
Strikes Back.
Looking at that list, it's pretty obvious
that I enjoy fantasy and science fiction.
Music is a big part of my daily life.
Whether I'm working on a project,
driving somewhere, playing games, or
relaxing, there's usually music playing
in the background.
My favourite artists at the moment are
The Weeknd, RAYE, and The Marías.
I also listen to a lot of game
soundtracks.
Some of my favourite gaming memories are
tied to specific songs.
I think music is one of the most
important parts of creating a memorable
experience.
One thing I have realised over the years
is that the projects and stories I
remember most are the ones that made me
feel genuinely excited.
The games, movies, music, and series I
enjoy have all inspired me in different
ways.
They have also influenced the kind of
creator I want to become.
In the future, I hope to create
experiences of my own that leave the same
kind of impact on other people.
Whether that's through games, stories, or
interactive projects, that's what
motivates me to keep creating.
One thing I have learned during my time
at Fontys is that ideas are only the
starting point of a project.
Coming up with concepts is something I
have always enjoyed, but turning those
ideas into something real is often much
more challenging.
Creating projects taught me that even the
best ideas need planning, iteration,
feedback, and a lot of patience before
they become successful outcomes.
Another important lesson I have learned
is the value of feedback.
In the past, I often kept working on
projects by myself for too long before
showing them to other people.
Over time I realised that feedback is one
of the fastest ways to improve a project.
While I still want to become better at
asking for feedback, I am much more
comfortable doing so now than I was at
the start of my studies.
Throughout my studies I have learned how
to work with a wide variety of tools and
software.
This semester alone I spent time working
with RPG Maker, Aseprite, DaVinci Fusion,
GitHub, moodboards, storyboards, and
research methods.
One thing I enjoy about creative work is
that there is always something new to
learn, and every project introduces a new
challenge.
Looking back, I think the biggest lesson
I have learned is that growth rarely
happens when everything goes according to
plan.
Many of the things I learned came from
mistakes, setbacks, technical problems,
or projects that did not go exactly the
way I expected.
While those moments can be frustrating,
they often end up teaching me more than
the successes.
Every project I complete leaves me with
new skills, new experiences, and new
ideas that I can take into the next one.
My goal is not simply to make games.
I want to create experiences that people
become invested in.
The stories, worlds, and characters that
have inspired me over the years are still
things I think about today.
One day, I hope to create something that
gives other people that same feeling.
Whether that is through games, stories,
or interactive experiences, that is what
motivates me to keep creating.
One thing I have learned over the years is
that I enjoy ambitious projects.
Many of my favourite ideas started as
concepts that felt far beyond my current
skill level.
Instead of seeing that as a reason not to
try, I try to see it as motivation to
keep learning.
Projects such as Threadweaver and SYNTHA
exist because I enjoy imagining things
that are larger than what I can currently
create.
Even if an idea takes years to fully
realise, I believe every step towards it
is worth taking.
One thing I enjoy about creative work is
that there is always something new to
learn.
In the future, I would like to learn
Blender, improve my programming skills,
create my own tilesets and artwork, and
become more confident with game
development tools.
While I was not able to learn Blender
during this semester, it remains one of
the skills I am most excited to develop.
The projects in this portfolio represent
what I have been working on so far.
While I am happy with what I have made,
there are still plenty of things I want
to improve.
I want to learn new tools, develop better
technical skills, and continue creating
larger and more ambitious projects.
For me, every project is another chance
to learn and improve as a creator.
